Defendants Adamas Builders Inc., Tony Digiorno, Adamas Development, LLC, Union And Polk Partners, LP, Andrew Broughton's Motion 1-To Compel Plaintiff Keehae Park's Appearance For Deposition, 2-To Compel Further Answers At Ms. Park's Deposition, 3-To Limit Plaintiff's Counsel To Objections Based On Privilege, And 4-For Monetary Sanctions Against Ms. Park And Her Counsel In The Amount Of $5,940 [For Entry Of Tentative Ruling Only] is off calendar as a duplicate entry. [302]

Notice Of Motion And Motion 1 To Compel Plaintiff Keehae Park S Appearance For Deposition 2 To Compel Further Answers At Ms. Park S Deposition 3 To Limit Plaintiffs Counsel To Objections Based On Privilege And 4 For Monetary Sanctions Against Ms. Park And Her Counsel In The Amount Of $5,940
Off Calendar Sep-16-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

There being no appearance, the tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

DEFENDANTS ADAMAS BUILDERS INC., TONY DIGIORNO, ADAMAS DEVELOPMENT, LLC, UNION AND POLK PARTNERS, LP, ANDREW BROUGHTON's Motion 1-To Compel Plaintiff Keehae Park's Appearance For Deposition, 2-To Compel Further Answers At Ms. Park's Deposition, 3-To Limit Plaintiff's Counsel To Objections Based On Privilege, And 4-For Monetary Sanctions Against Ms. Park And Her Counsel In The Amount Of $5,940.

The motion to compel is GRANTED AND DENIED IN PART. Ms. Park must sit for a continued deposition. Date should be set by agreement: Defendants have not shown why it is "critical" for her deposition to conclude before the "series of depositions" starting 9/26 (MPA at 13). There is nothing improper about Ms. Park's use of an interpreter. Counsel may not instruct a witness to not answer unless based on privilege or other recognized basis, when the question calls for a legal contention, or the questions unreasonably annoy, embarrass, or oppress the deponent. CCP 20225.460 (privilege must be claimed) 2025.470 (permitting suspension of deposition to move for order to protect against bad faith or harassing conduct); Stewart v. Colonial Western Agency (2001) 87 Cal.App.4th 1006 (if questions are harassing, the party should suspend and seek protective order); Rifkind v. Sup. Ct. (1994) (legal contention questions may be inappropriate at deposition). Turning to the questions in the Separate Statement. 1/Q at 12:12-22 - objections sustained - asked and answered at 11:1-18. 2/Q at 29:7-13, 3/Q at 29:19-24; 4/Q at 31:17-32:6 - objections overruled - the word "allegedly," as used in these questions, is not so argumentative that the questions cannot fairly be answered. 4/Q at 31:17-32:6 - while compound is not an appropriate basis to instruct not to answer, the question is compound and confusing. 5/Q at 39:11-17 - motion to compel denied - at 39:18 Plaintiff answered despite the instruction not to answer. Defendants did not move to compel further answers to the questions about the consequences of perjury, so the Court will not order further testimony as urged in the Reply. Moreover, Ms. Park testified that she understood she could be punished if she "state[s] something [that] is not true." 13:21-23. Plaintiffs' request to appoint a discovery special master is denied. This request should be raised in a properly noticed CCP 639 motion and not in an opposition. The requests for sanctions by both parties are DENIED because the motion is granted in part and denied in part.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the ruling and must submit it in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge Pro Tem: Scott Borrowman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.
